title: Global growth in solar "the largest ever observed for any source"
description: The Energy Information Agency says we've entered the Age of Electricity.

title: This Scammer Used an AI-Generated MAGA Girl to Grift ‘Super Dumb’ Men
description: A med student says he’s made thousands of dollars selling photos and videos of a young conservative woman he created using generative tools. He’s not alone.

title: Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past
description: In the past, even with an icebreaker and during peak melt season, getting to the North Pole wasn’t a sure bet. It took favorable winds to crack the frozen ocean surface, and ships had to fight through ice that had grown many meters thick over several winters. In the summer of 2025, though, Jochen Kn

title: Pentagon pulls the plug on one of the military's most troubled space programs
description: Problems with the ground system would have "put current GPS military and civilian capabilities at risk."

title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
description: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all of these countries except Japan.

title: John Ternus will replace Tim Cook as Apple CEO
description: Cook will be executive chairman, but will no longer run the company day to day.

title: Absurd study suggests eating fruits and vegetables leads to cancer
description: Experts point out a series of flaws, including small size and no control group.

title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
description: This sentence construction ("It's not just this — it's that") has become so common in AI-generated writing that it's no longer just a clue that a piece of writing may be synthetic — it's almost a guarantee.

title: US opens refund portal to start paying back Trump's illegal tariffs
description: Importers can now request refunds, two months after Trump's Supreme Court loss.

title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.

title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Tech CEOs Think AI Will Let Them Be Everywhere at Once
description: Mark Zuckerberg and Jack Dorsey have different visions for how to use AI for management purposes, but both imagine a system of heightened control.

title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
description: If you want to capture something wolflike, it’s best to embark before dawn. So on a morning this January, with the eastern horizon still pink-hued, I drove with two young scientists into a blanket of fog. Forty miles to the west, the industrial sprawl of Houston spawned a golden glow. Tanner Broussa

title: Chinese tech workers are starting to train their AI doubles–and pushing back
description: Tech workers in China are being instructed by their bosses to train AI agents to replace them—and it’s prompting a wave of soul-searching among otherwise enthusiastic early adopters.  Earlier this month a GitHub project called Colleague Skill, which claimed workers could use it to “distill” the

title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App
description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.

title: Pie Day 2026
description: Ellie’s Pi Day post: https://mitadmissions.org/blogs/entry/pi-day-2026-food-institute/ How Ellie orchestrated the baking of 30 pies: https://mitadmissions.org/blogs/entry/behind-the-scenes-of-thirty-pies/

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
